# Fuenzer Sports

Fuenzer Sports is an AI-driven sports analytics simulation platform. It functions as a hybrid between an instant search interface and the Football Manager game.

Users simply type a natural language question regarding a tournament (e.g., "What are Spain's chances of passing the group stage in the 2026 World Cup?"), and the engine runs thousands of mathematical Monte Carlo simulations in seconds.

## Main Navigation

- [Home](https://sports.fuenzer.web.id/) - Run instant sports analytics and tournament simulations.
- [Terms of Service](https://sports.fuenzer.web.id/terms) - Read the terms of service governing this tournament simulator.
- [Privacy Policy](https://sports.fuenzer.web.id/privacy) - Learn about our privacy policy and analytics data handling.

## Crawler & SEO Resources

- [Sitemap](https://sports.fuenzer.web.id/sitemap.xml) - XML sitemap of indexable routes.
- [Robots](https://sports.fuenzer.web.id/robots.txt) - Search engine crawler directives.

## Tech Stack & Architecture

- **Frontend:** React 18, TypeScript, Zustand (persisted to localStorage), Framer Motion. Fully stateless UI hosted on Cloudflare Pages.
- **Backend:** Python, FastAPI, NumPy (vectorized Monte Carlo). Fully stateless compute hosted on Hugging Face Spaces.
- **Core Engine:** Prompt-driven World Cup 2026 group stage simulation with Round of 32 greedy matching algorithm.
- **Strict Dark Mode:** Adheres to a strict dark UI aesthetic.

## Project Details
- **Domain:** https://sports.fuenzer.web.id
- **Target Audience:** Football enthusiasts, sports bettors, and data analysts.
- **Problem Solved:** Traditional sports analytics are static and difficult to query. Fuenzer Sports provides a dynamic natural language interface to run complex Monte Carlo simulations on the fly.

## Key Features
- **Prompt-Driven Simulations:** "Stitch-style" search box to query tournament outcomes.
- **Stateless AI Architecture:** Chat sessions are stored client-side. The backend only performs mathematical computation and returns probabilities.
- **Knockout Bracket & Greedy Algorithm:** Efficient O(1) matching algorithm for 3rd-place teams in the Round of 32 (avoiding hardcoding 495 permutations).
